steps: Navigate to the app with ?modal=billing-plan appended to the URL
Verify the full-screen billing plan modal opens
Close the modal
Verify the ?modal=billing-plan param is removed from the URL
tests.setup: Ensure the org is on a paid plan so the billing modal is dismissible.
tests.expected: Billing plan modal opens on page load. Closing it removes the modal param from the URL.

tests.description: ?modal=settings&tab=billing opens settings on the billing tab
steps: Navigate to the app with ?modal=settings&tab=billing in the URL
Verify the Settings modal opens
Verify the Billing tab is active (not Account or Organization)
Close the modal
Verify modal params are removed from the URL
tests.expected: Settings modal opens directly on the Billing tab. URL is cleaned on close.

tests.description: ?modal=host-editor&id=<uuid>&tab=services opens the host editor for that host on the services tab
steps: Navigate to the Hosts tab and note the ID of an existing host (from the URL or network inspector)
Reload the page with ?modal=host-editor&id=<that-uuid>&tab=services in the URL
Verify the host editor modal opens for the correct host
Verify the Services tab is active inside the editor
tests.setup: Create at least one host with some services so the Services tab has content. Use the API: POST /api/hosts with a name and network_id.
tests.expected: Host editor opens for the specified host with the Services tab selected.

steps: Navigate to the app with ?modal=support in the URL
Verify the Support modal opens
Close the modal
Verify modal params are removed from the URL
tests.expected: Support modal opens on page load and URL is cleaned on close.

tests.description: ?modal=create-daemon opens the create daemon modal and switches to daemons tab
steps: Navigate to the app with ?modal=create-daemon in the URL
Verify the sidebar switches to the Daemons tab
Verify the Create Daemon modal opens
tests.setup: Delete all daemons so the tab has none (the modal only auto-opens when there are no daemons).
tests.expected: App navigates to daemons tab and opens the create daemon modal.

tests.description: Opening a modal via button click updates the URL with modal params
steps: Click the Settings gear icon in the sidebar
Verify the URL now contains ?modal=settings
Click the Billing tab inside settings
Verify the URL now contains ?modal=settings&tab=billing
Close the modal
Verify all modal params are removed from the URL
tests.expected: URL reflects the current modal state at every step. Opening adds params, tab switches update the tab param, closing removes all params.

tests.description: Opening an entity modal via click updates the URL with name and entity ID
steps: Navigate to the Hosts tab
Click on an existing host to edit it
Verify the URL contains ?modal=host-editor&id=<host-uuid>
Close the host editor
Verify modal params are removed from the URL
tests.setup: Create at least one host via the API.
tests.expected: URL includes the modal name and entity ID while the modal is open.

tests.description: Upgrade button in sidebar opens billing plan modal via new system
steps: Look for the Upgrade button in the sidebar (visible for Free plan owners)
Click the Upgrade button
Verify the billing plan modal opens
Verify the URL contains ?modal=billing-plan
tests.setup: Org should be on the Free plan with billing enabled.
tests.expected: Upgrade button opens billing modal and URL reflects the modal state.

tests.description: Settings > Billing > View Plans opens billing modal, closing it reopens settings on billing tab
steps: Open Settings modal
Navigate to the Billing tab
Click 'View Plans'
Verify the Settings modal closes and the Billing Plan modal opens
Close the Billing Plan modal
Verify the Settings modal reopens on the Billing tab
tests.expected: Full round-trip: Settings → Billing Plan → back to Settings on Billing tab.

tests.description: Completing onboarding navigates to main app with daemon setup modal
steps: Complete the full onboarding flow (register, org setup, use case)
After onboarding completes, verify you land on the main app
Verify the Daemons tab is active
Verify the Create Daemon modal is open
tests.expected: Onboarding completion navigates to main app with daemon setup modal open. No sessionStorage is used.

tests.description: Past-due org auto-opens non-dismissible settings on billing tab
steps: Load the app
Verify the Settings modal opens automatically on the Billing tab
Verify the modal cannot be dismissed (no close button, clicking outside does nothing)
tests.setup: Set the org to past_due status: UPDATE organizations SET plan_status = 'past_due' WHERE id = '<org_id>'. Refresh the browser.
tests.expected: Settings modal opens on Billing tab automatically and cannot be dismissed.

tests.description: OIDC account link callback opens settings modal (was ?auth_modal, now ?modal=settings)
steps: Go to Settings > Account tab
Click 'Link' next to an OIDC provider (e.g., Google)
Complete the OIDC flow
Verify you return to the app with the Settings modal open
tests.expected: After OIDC callback, Settings modal opens via ?modal=settings in the return URL.

tests.description: Closing any named modal clears all modal params without leaving stale state
steps: Open a host editor for a specific host (URL should show ?modal=host-editor&id=...&tab=...)
Close the modal
Verify the URL has no modal, id, or tab params
Open the Support modal
Close it
Verify URL is clean again
tests.expected: No stale modal/id/tab params remain in URL after closing any modal.

steps: Note the ID of an existing network
Navigate to the app with ?modal=network-editor&id=<uuid> in the URL
Verify the network editor modal opens for the correct network
tests.setup: Ensure at least one network exists (one is created during onboarding by default).
tests.expected: Network edit modal opens for the specified network.

tests.description: ?modal=group-editor&id=<uuid>&tab=bindings opens group editor on bindings tab
steps: Note the ID of an existing group
Navigate with ?modal=group-editor&id=<uuid>&tab=bindings
Verify the group editor opens for the correct group
Verify the Bindings tab is active
tests.setup: Create a group via the Groups tab or API.
tests.expected: Group editor opens on the Bindings tab for the specified group.
